label: Combat OS — Foundation / Corrective (Phase 1, 4 weeks)
summary: Rebuild the strength and joint control hard training often misses, so you can return to heavier work with confidence.
outcomes: Restore hip and pelvic stability without heavy spinal loading
Build a stronger trunk, shoulders and single-leg base
Maintain useful strength while recovery remains the priority
description: A 4-week spine-friendly foundation block for a lean, advanced striker returning to full training after chiropractic spinal adjustments (cleared to train). Goal: rebuild the muscles striking neglects — glutes/pelvic stability, deep anti-movement core, rotator cuff, mid/lower traps, single-leg stability — while maintaining strength. Loads capped at RPE 7 on joint-friendly machine and single-leg lifts; no heavy axial barbell work. Run alongside heavy sport practice and yoga; if sessions feel short, bank the time in the recovery studio. Graduate to combatos-operator-2026 (Phase 2).

days.label: Day 1 — Legs, Glutes & Deep Core
days.focus: Leg strength without spinal load, glute/pelvic stability, deep core

items.name: 45° Leg Press
items.cue: Heavy but controlled, full range, mid-foot drive. Loads your legs hard with zero spinal compression.

items.name: Single-Leg Glute Machine (kickback)
items.cue: Elbows supported, full extension, hard squeeze and a beat's pause. Strong glutes = a stable pelvis and a protected lower back.

items.name: Single-Leg RDL (DB, light)
items.cue: Light — hip control and balance, not load. Teaches the clean hinge your spine wants.

items.name: Dead Bug
items.cue: Slow, ribs down, low back glued to the floor. Deep-core anti-extension is priority #1 post-adjustment.

days.label: Day 2 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 3 — Push, Pull & Shoulder Health
days.focus: Spine-friendly pulling, maintained pressing, rotator-cuff & trap rebuild

items.name: Chest-Supported Row (plate-loaded)
items.cue: Chest on the pad — zero lower-back demand. Squeeze the shoulder blades, control the return.

items.name: Machine Shoulder Press
items.cue: Machine keeps it stable this month. Maintains pressing strength without axial load.

items.name: Cable Face Pull
items.cue: The area you flagged as weak. High elbows, pull to the eyes, pause. Do it religiously.

items.name: Cable External Rotation
items.cue: Light, elbow pinned to your side, slow. Direct rotator-cuff health.

days.label: Day 4 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 5 — Single-Leg, Pull & Carries
days.focus: Single-leg strength & symmetry, stable vertical pull, anti-rotation & carry core

items.name: Reverse Lunge (DB, controlled)
items.cue: Controlled and balanced, drive through the front heel. Single-leg strength with minimal spinal load.

items.name: Lat Pulldown (machine)
items.cue: Machine-stable vertical pulling — keeps your strong lats strong while sparing grip strain this month.

items.name: Pallof Press (cable)
items.cue: Resist the rotation, don't create it. Protects the spine and builds punch-transferable core.

items.name: Farmer Carry (DB)
items.cue: Heavy DBs, stand tall and braced, walk slow. The most spine-friendly core builder there is. Use the turf track.

days.label: Day 6 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 7 — Pick / Recovery
days.focus: Yoga or mobility class, a recovery-studio session (cryo / infrared sauna / plunge), or a bonus session. Log what you did.
